title: NOC 72404 – Aircraft mechanics and aircraft inspectors | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Aircraft Mechanics and Aircraft Inspectors (NOC 72404) are the critical safety experts powering Canada’s revitalized aviation sector and the new National Defence Industrial Strategy. With Canada increasing its defense spending and commercial air travel reaching new peaks, these professionals are essential for the maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) of various aircraft. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they ensure airworthiness through rigorous mechanical and structural inspections. In 2026, the role has integrated Digital Twin technology and advanced composite repair techniques to handle the newest generations of fuel-efficient and hybrid aircraft.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Troubleshooting & Repair: Troubleshooting, repairing, and overhauling aircraft structural, mechanical, and hydraulic systems.
Routine Maintenance: Adjusting and repairing airframes, engines, and other aircraft systems according to 2026 Transport Canada regulations.
Inspection & Certification: Inspecting aircraft and systems to verify they meet performance and safety standards, and signing maintenance releases to certify airworthiness.
Documentation: Maintaining detailed logs of all maintenance and repair work performed to ensure a clear safety trail.
Avionics Support: (For dual-rated technicians) Testing and repairing electronic components and navigation systems.
employment_requirements: Education: A university degree or college diploma in Aircraft Maintenance or a related field is typically required.
Licensing: An Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (AME) license issued by Transport Canada is mandatory for signing maintenance releases and certifying airworthiness.
Experience: Several years of on-the-job training are required. For the AME license, specific logged hours and passing the Transport Canada exams are mandatory.
Certification: Specialized certification is available from the Canadian Council for Aviation & Aerospace (CCAA) for various technical roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Very Good, driven by a severe skilled-worker shortage in both the commercial and military sectors:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72404 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was as low as 50, reflecting the province's urgent need for mechanics to support its massive aerospace cluster.

Alberta: High Demand. Alberta offers some of the highest wages for this NOC, with average annual salaries in Calgary and Edmonton reaching $82,018 to $91,563, particularly for those working with major carriers like WestJet or specialized MRO firms.
British Columbia: Strong Demand. Aircraft mechanics are currently on the BC PNP Priority Occupations List. Candidates with a job offer benefit from targeted provincial draws and faster processing.

National Trend: The federal government’s 2026 budget includes an 85% increase in defense and research investment, projected to create up to 125,000 new defense-related jobs, many of which require certified aircraft technicians.
Why Work in Canada as an Aircraft Mechanic?
Lead the Aviation Revolution: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including AI-driven predictive maintenance sensors and advanced avionics integration.
Unmatched Sector Stability: Work for international airlines, private MRO facilities, or the federal government’s defense services.
Elite Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your median hourly wage in Canada is $38.42/hr, with experienced inspectors in high-demand regions earning over $134,000 annually.
Major 2026 PR Priority: As of February 18, 2026, NOC 72404 is a core occupation in the newly redesigned Express Entry "Transport" Category, which targets aviation professionals specifically.
pathways: Express Entry (2026 Transport Category): Launched on February 18, 2026, this category targets NOC 72404. Candidates require 12 months of qualifying experience (Canadian or international) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA at lower CRS scores.
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72404 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream with scores starting at 50.
BC PNP Priority Construction/Transport: Provides priority processing and lower score requirements for aircraft mechanics with a valid BC job offer.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Aircraft mechanics are classified as "High-Priority Technical Infrastructure Workers."
